narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes Gore's climate policy by implying it unfairly targets poor people and ignores the benefits of cheap and abundant energy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes Al Gore, an authority figure in the climate movement, by highlighting his perceived hypocrisy in using large amounts of fossil fuels despite advocating for their reduction.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote downplays the significance of climate change by suggesting that past predictions of catastrophic events have not come true, implying that current warnings are also exaggerated or unfounded.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote questions the accuracy of climate science by pointing out the alleged failure of past predictions, implying that the scientific consensus is flawed or unreliable.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes the climate movement by using the pejorative term 'cultists' and implying that their language is exaggerated or manipulative.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for “renewable energy,” meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote expresses skepticism about green technologies by implying that they are not viable or desirable, and that investing in them will lead to a 'dark and cold future'.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Unless America and all other sovereign nations open up their borders to an illegal alien onslaught, the climate crisis will only continue to worsen, Gore went on to allege.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote implies that there is a hidden plot to use the climate crisis as a pretext for opening borders and allowing an 'illegal alien onslaught', suggesting a secret scheme by powerful groups to undermine national sovereignty.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes Gore's climate policy by implying it unfairly targets poor people and ignores the benefits of cheap and abundant energy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'cultists' and implying that their language is exaggerated or manipulative, suggesting that the movement is driven by ideology rather than science.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote expresses skepticism about green technologies by implying that they are not viable or desirable, and that investing in them will lead to a 'dark and cold future'.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Unless America and all other sovereign nations open up their borders to an illegal alien onslaught, the climate crisis will only continue to worsen, Gore went on to allege.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote implies that there is a hidden plot to use the climate crisis as a pretext for opening borders and undermining national sovereignty, suggesting that powerful groups are manipulating the climate narrative for their own purposes.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes Gore's climate policy by implying it unfairly targets poor people and ignores the benefits of cheap and abundant energy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote challenges Gore's integrity and credibility on climate issues by highlighting his personal use of fossil fuels, implying hypocrisy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote downplays the significance of climate change by suggesting that past predictions have been incorrect and exaggerated, implying current warnings are also unfounded.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote raises doubts about the scientific consensus and predictions on climate change by citing a claimed perfect failure rate of past predictions, questioning the reliability of climate science.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'cultists' and implying that their language is exaggerated and scare-mongering, challenging their motives and impact.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for “renewable energy,” meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote expresses skepticism about green technologies by implying that investing in renewable energy will lead to a negative outcome for humanity, suggesting that these technologies are not viable or beneficial.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Unless America and all other sovereign nations open up their borders to an illegal alien onslaught, the climate crisis will only continue to worsen, Gore went on to allege.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote implies a hidden plot or agenda related to climate change, suggesting that Gore's real goal is to facilitate an 'illegal alien onslaught' rather than genuinely addressing environmental concerns.
